title: Governor Murphy Announces Zero-Emission Vehicle Report to Accompany Strategic Investments
content: Governor Phil Murphy highlighted investments in decarbonizing and electrifying the transportation sector, based on recommendations outlined in the New Jersey Zero-Emission Vehicle (ZEV) Roadmap report. Developed by New Jerseyâ€™s Partnership to Plug-in, the roadmap affirms the need to maintain ZEV incentives, enhance infrastructure, and prioritize equity. To demonstrate this commitment, the Administration announced significant investments, including $75.5 million for Phase 3 of the New Jersey Zero-Emission Incentive Program (NJ ZIP), $25 million for the NJ ZEV Financing Program, and $50 million for the Take Charge pilot program. Additionally, $16 million is allocated for NJ TRANSIT electric bus infrastructure. The report reflects on the exponential increase in adoption, with over 280,000 EVs on the road by November 2025. The Administration also unveiled a One-Stop Shop online interactive tool to help residents and local governments explore funding for green projects, connecting them with federal, state, and utility programs.
author: Analysts Account
